# Assets Structure Audit

**Generated by hand 2026-07-19** (ASSETS & PROMPTS AUDIT round, owner
order — "čini mi se da se ne držimo uvek pravila"). A full walk of
`assets/`, cross-referenced against `config/defaults.py`,
`config/archetypes.py`, `config/constants.py` and the code paths that
actually consume each file. **Audit only** — nothing under `assets/`
was moved, renamed or deleted; every candidate below is a LIST, for
the owner to decide.

## Executive Summary

- **The owner's instinct was right.** Three real, code-visible
  inconsistencies confirmed: (1) filename CASE varies by theme with no
  discernible rule (some themes Capitalize, some don't — not even
  consistently themed by pantheon vs. planetary); (2) a whole staging
  folder, `assets/EXTRA/` (60 files), sits parallel to the real trees
  and is read by NOTHING in code — pure orphan risk; (3) a genuine
  **stale-vs-live naming split** inside the season badges: the code
  reads `Wet_Season.png` / `Summer_Solstice.png` (underscored) while
  `WetSeason.png` / `SummerSolstice.png` (no underscore) sit beside
  them, unreferenced by any `.py` file — and
  [Roster](../ROSTER.md)'s own generator (`build_roster.py`) is
  tracking the WRONG (dead) stem name, so its ✔ marks for those rows
  are misleading. See §Completeness for the exact list.
- **The `archetype/` and `weekday/` trees are the most disciplined**
  in the repo: every theme is `<source>/<family>/<variant>`, `dual/`
  always nests inside its variant, `colored/` always siblings
  `primary`/whichever variant it recolors. `badge/` and `emblem/` are
  slightly looser (see below) but not broken.
- **The dual question:** flattening `dual/` subfolders to flat files +
  the config mapping that ALREADY EXISTS (`WEEKDAY_DUAL_FILES`, a
  plain dict of path strings) is a SMALL, mechanical change — not a
  structural rework. Full cost/benefit in §The Dual Question;
  recommendation: **flatten**, low cost, real (if modest) gain, and it
  matches the pattern the Pantheon roster already uses
  (`WEEKDAY_PANTHEON["files"]`, candidate paths as plain strings with
  no dedicated subfolder).

## Table of Contents

- [Structure Inconsistencies](#structure)
- [Completeness](#completeness)
- [The Dual Question](#dual-question)

<a id="structure"></a>

## Structure Inconsistencies

### 1. Filename case has no consistent rule

Confirmed by direct listing (`assets/weekday/gemini/<theme>/primary/`):

| Capitalized | lowercase |
|---|---|
| greek (`Helios`, `Zeus`…), norse (`Odin`, `Thor`…), profession (`Artist`, `Farmer`…), bee (`Queen`, `Drone`…), elephant (`Matriarch`…), bee/elephant/wolf colored variants | egypt (`ra`, `khonsu`…), slavic (`dazbog`, `perun`…), alchemy (`gold`, `iron`…), japan (`nichiyobi`…), religion (`christianity`…), cosmos (`sun`, `nebula`…), wolf primary (`alpha`, `luna`…), bible/bible2/bible_dark (`abraham`, `lucifer`…) |

This is CODED intentionally as `config.defaults._LOWERCASE_THEMES` —
so it is not an accident the app breaks on, but it is genuinely
arbitrary from a content standpoint (there is no religion/mythology/
register rule that predicts which list a theme lands in — Greek and
Norse gods are capitalized, Egyptian and Slavic gods are not, for no
stated reason beyond "the owner's art uses lowercase stems" on a
per-theme basis). Not a bug; a housekeeping nit the owner may want to
level in a future asset-naming pass — flagging, not fixing (Rule #6
territory if changed: every `WEEKDAY_THEME_FILES` override would need
updating in lockstep).

### 2. `assets/EXTRA/` — an unreferenced staging tree, 60 files

`grep -rn "EXTRA" research/build_roster.py` and a full `.py` grep for
the literal string `EXTRA` (as a path segment) both come back empty —
**nothing in the application or the roster generator ever reads this
folder.** Contents are alternate/earlier drops: duplicate copies of
files that already exist properly under `archetype/<source>/family/`
(same filenames — `Child_Dawn.png`, `Hearth.png`, etc., byte-identical
candidates worth a diff before any cleanup), lowercase duplicates of
Capitalized canonical stems (`elephant/elder.png` beside the real
`elephant/Elder.png`), raw ChatGPT/Gemini export filenames never
renamed (`ChatGPT Image Jul 15, 2026, 04_20_49 PM.png`), and versioned
alternates (`scale glass v2/judas_v1.png` / `judas_v2.png` — the real
scale-glass folder already resolved this via the `_v2` SUFFIX
convention `assets/badge/chatgpt/scale/glass/` uses instead of a
sibling folder). **Candidate for archival OUTSIDE `assets/`** (a
`raw_drops/` or similar at the repo root, well away from the
`<collection>/<source>/...` convention every other folder honors) —
never delete without the owner's own look, per Rule #3 in the root
CLAUDE.md; some of these may be the ONLY copy of an alternate take the
owner still wants to compare.

### 3. `badge/season/` — dead-stem duplicates, and Roster is tracking the wrong one

Both sources ship BOTH of these pairs side by side:

- `WetSeason.png` (dead) beside `Wet_Season.png` (live)
- `DrySeason.png` (dead) beside `Dry_Season.png` (live)
- `turning_point/SummerSolstice.png` (dead) beside `turning_point/
  Summer_Solstice.png` (live)
- `turning_point/WinterSolstice.png` (dead) beside `turning_point/
  Winter_Solstice.png` (live)

"Live" here means: `app/encyclopedia.py` (`_SEASON_ENTRIES`,
`_SUN_ENTRIES`, the `topics["seasons"]`/`topics["sun"]` builders) reads
ONLY the underscored stems — `Wet_Season.png`, `Summer_Solstice.png`,
etc. A full `.py` grep for the no-underscore stems (`WetSeason`,
`DrySeason`, `SummerSolstice`, `WinterSolstice`) returns **zero
matches anywhere in the codebase.** Those four no-underscore files (×2
sources = 8 files) are pure dead weight today.

**This also means [Roster](../ROSTER.md) is stale on this point**:
`build_roster.py` line ~308/313 checks the NO-underscore stems
(`"WetSeason", "DrySeason"`, `"SummerSolstice", "WinterSolstice"`) —
the exact ones the app does NOT read — so its ✔ marks for those four
Badges rows are technically checking dead files, not the live ones the
app actually shows. The live underscored files happen to also exist
(confirmed on disk), so the APP itself is fine — but the roster's
audit trail for these four rows is checking the wrong filename. Worth
a `build_roster.py` fix in a future session (out of scope for this
audit — reporting only, per the task brief).

### 4. Bible family shares ONE root; Religion family does the same — consistent, worth noting as the pattern

`bible`/`bible2`/`bible_dark` all live under one `weekday/bible/
{primary,secondary,dark}/` tree, and `religion`/`religion_alt` under
one `weekday/religion/{primary,secondary}/` tree — this is the
documented FAMILY/VARIANT convention (`config.defaults.
WEEKDAY_THEME_DIRS` comment: "related themes share a family"). Not a
deviation; flagging only because it is the one place two visibly
DIFFERENT theme names (`bible2` reads nothing like "secondary" without
the doc) map onto directory segments that don't spell the theme name —
harmless today, a small trap for a future agent grepping for
`bible2/` and finding nothing.

### 5. `variant subfolders present in some themes, absent in others where the shape would fit`

`colored/` exists for every theme with a colored register (greek,
norse, egypt, slavic pantheons excepted — pantheons have NO colored
folder at all, only primary bronze; consistent with ROSTER's Pantheon
table, which never shows a Colored G/C column for pantheon rows — not
a gap, the pantheon roster was never scoped to ship colored art).
`pantheon/` exists only for greek/norse/egypt/slavic (the four themes
with a documented Pantheon roster) — consistent with
`config.defaults.WEEKDAY_PANTHEON`'s four keys. No orphan `pantheon/`
or `colored/` folder found sitting next to a theme that does not use
it. This axis is clean.

<a id="completeness"></a>

## Completeness

Per-collection missing files beyond what [Roster](../ROSTER.md)
already tracks (weekday/zodiac/badge-emblem) — see
[COVERAGE.md](prompts/COVERAGE.md) for the full archetype/era/subdial
breakdown (Task 1 of this audit). Summary of the highest-value gaps:

| Collection | Gap | Priority reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| `era/` | 100% missing, both sources | Sheet complete, zero art — the single biggest all-or-nothing hole found |
| `badge/subdial/` | 3 of 4 light-variant masters missing (`south`, `h3`, `h21`) | Not even tracked as a Roster shortage row today |
| `archetype/*/evangelist/` | 100% missing, both sources | Sheet complete; currently degrades gracefully (name-only fallback), so low urgency despite being 0% |
| `archetype/chatgpt/temperaments/Throne.png` | 1 file | CENTER art — visible every solar noon/midnight window, not a peripheral seat |
| `archetype/chatgpt/persons/Seal.png` | 1 file | Same — a center image |
| `badge/season/{WetSeason,DrySeason,turning_point/SummerSolstice,turning_point/WinterSolstice}.png` (×2 sources) | 8 dead files | Not a generation gap — a CLEANUP candidate (§Structure #3) |

<a id="dual-question"></a>

## The Dual Question

**The owner's question:** why do `dual/` subfolders exist at all
instead of flat files + a JSON/config mapping — which the config
ALREADY has?

**What already exists today (verified in `config/defaults.py`):**

- `WEEKDAY_DUAL_FILES` (line ~1546) is ALREADY exactly that mapping —
  a flat `{theme: "path/string"}` dict, one string per theme, that
  happens to have `/dual/` baked into the middle of each string
  (`"greek/primary/dual/Phaethon"`, `"bible": "bible/primary/dual/
  son_servant"`, 19 entries total covering every dual-bearing theme).
  The FOLDER is not doing any structural work the dict doesn't already
  do — it is a naming convention living inside a string, not a second
  code path.
- The Pantheon roster (`WEEKDAY_PANTHEON["dual"]`, 4 entries) proves
  the flat pattern already works in this exact codebase: its dual
  candidates are plain tuples of path strings with NO subfolder
  segment forcing a dedicated tree (`"norse/primary/Odin"` — no
  `dual/` at all, it reuses the sun seat's own file).
- `WEEKDAY_DUAL_NAMES` (hover title text) is independent of the file
  layout entirely — already flat, already just names.

**What would change if `dual/` folders went flat (e.g.
`greek/primary/dual/Phaethon.png` → `greek/primary/Phaethon.png`):**

1. **`WEEKDAY_DUAL_FILES`** — 19 string edits, delete the `/dual`
   segment from each. Mechanical, one file, ~5 minutes.
2. **Prompt sheets** — `grep -c "dual/"` across `research/prompts/
   weekday/*.md` found 18 files carrying the literal `dual/` segment
   in their drop-path lines (dozens of occurrences total, since each
   dual entry states its path twice: the arrow line and the fenced
   prompt reference). Every sheet needing a byte-identical path
   update — mechanical find-replace per file, but 18 files is real
   editing work, not a one-liner.
3. **Files already on disk** — every theme that HAS generated dual art
   today would need its file physically moved up one directory level
   (`git mv`), for both sources. No filename COLLISIONS are possible
   (every dual stem — `Phaethon`, `Skoll`, `afu_ra`, `dazbog_old`,
   `ore`, `ama_no_iwato`, `satanism`, `corax`, `Servant`, `omega`,
   `Cleaner`, `Memory`, `son_servant`, `isaac`, `judas`, `black_hole`,
   `sun_eclipse` — is already unique within its variant folder, so
   flattening never overwrites a sibling seat file). Roughly 30–40
   files across both sources today (fewer than the ROSTER shortage
   list suggests, since several duals are still missing art).
4. **The COLORED dual convention** — `colored/dual/<stem>.png` follows
   the same pattern one level down and would flatten the same way
   (`colored/<stem>.png`), no extra complexity.
5. **PromptPainter's own output** — the tool writes exactly the path
   named in the sheet's arrow line; it has no independent opinion
   about `dual/` as a folder, so item 2 (editing the sheets) is the
   ONLY PromptPainter-side change needed — no tool code changes.
6. **Render/lookup code** — `pantheon_seat()` and every dual-resolving
   helper already just does `WEEKDAY_ART_DIR / f"{rel}.png"` on
   whatever string sits in the config dict; NOTHING in the render
   layer branches on the literal word `"dual"` — the folder carries
   zero semantic weight at runtime, confirmed by grep (no `"dual" in
   path` or similar string check anywhere in `render/`).

**What is gained:** one fewer directory level to navigate per theme
when the owner drops art by hand (mild), a slightly shorter drop path
in every prompt sheet (mild), and REMOVING an asymmetry — pantheon
duals are ALREADY flat (`WEEKDAY_PANTHEON`) while planetary duals are
not, so today's rule is "flat, except when it's the Sunday dual,
except when it's a pantheon dual" — three different conventions for
what is conceptually the same kind of seat. Flattening the planetary
duals would leave exactly ONE convention (flat file + config string)
everywhere in the weekday system.

**What is lost / risked:** the `dual/` folder currently makes it
visually obvious, scrolling a file browser, which file is "the eighth
seat, not one of the seven" — useful when the owner is dropping art
by hand and wants a quick visual grouping. That legibility moves
entirely into the prompt sheet and the config dict once flattened (no
folder to eyeball).

**Recommendation: flatten it.** The cost is real but bounded and
one-time (18 sheet files + ~35 disk files + one config dict edit); the
gain is a single, uniform convention across the ENTIRE weekday system
instead of three overlapping ones, which is exactly the kind of
inconsistency this audit was commissioned to surface. This is the
owner's call to make, not this audit's — recorded here as a
recommendation with the cost quantified, per the task brief.
